metadata.descriptionSuffix: Private in-browser processing, no watermark on standard output, no forced account for typical use.

actions.compare: compare two PDFs side by side
actions.booklet: impose a PDF as a print-ready booklet
actions.safeShare: audit a PDF for sensitive content before sharing
actions.paperMargin: set custom paper size and margins on a PDF
actions.pageNumbers: add page numbers to a PDF
actions.crop: crop a PDF in your browser
actions.watermark: add a text watermark to a PDF
actions.rotate: rotate PDF pages in your browser
actions.autocad: convert AutoCAD DXF drawings to PDF
actions.iwork: convert Apple iWork files to PDF
actions.passwordRecovery: recover a short forgotten PDF password
actions.flatten: flatten a PDF and remove editable layers
actions.removeMetadata: remove hidden PDF metadata before sharing
actions.sign: sign a PDF with your signature
actions.pngToPdf: convert PNG images into a PDF
actions.heicToPdf: convert HEIC photos into a PDF
actions.pdfToJpg: export PDF pages as JPG images
actions.pdfToPng: export PDF pages as PNG images

guide.variantP1: This page helps you {action} when your search intent is "{keyword}". You get the same JoinMyPDF experience as our main {toolTitle} flow—clear steps, local processing in your browser, and a straightforward download when you are done.
guide.defaultP1: {description} Whether you are packaging invoices, preparing coursework, or sending a contract pack, the goal is simple: {action} without installing desktop software.
guide.p2: Privacy is a product decision here: your files are handled in the browser session on your device rather than uploaded to JoinMyPDF servers for processing. That model reduces transfer time for many jobs and is easier to reason about when documents are sensitive.
guide.variantP3: Because you landed on a page tuned for "{keyword}", we emphasize {angle} If you ever want the generic experience, you can always switch to the main {toolTitle} page from the navigation.
guide.defaultP3: If you need related steps next, many people follow merge with compression for email limits, or split first when only a few pages matter. Internal links on this page point to adjacent tools so you can continue in one sitting.
guide.p4WithSecondary: Helpful related phrases people use alongside this task include {secondary}. You do not need to worry about matching exact wording—follow the on-page checklist, confirm the preview looks right, and download.
guide.p4Default: Follow the on-page checklist, confirm previews where available, and download once the status line shows success.
guide.p5Default: If you are new to browser-based PDF tools, start with a small test file, verify the output, then run your real documents.
guide.p6: If something fails, it is usually a browser memory limit on very large files, a mixed set of inputs, or a protected PDF. Try fewer pages per run, export from the original authoring app again, or split the document before converting.

faqs.freeA: Yes. JoinMyPDF does not require payment for standard in-browser processing of everyday documents.
faqs.uploadQ: Do my files get uploaded to JoinMyPDF servers?
faqs.uploadA: These tools process files in your browser session. That helps keep sensitive documents off our infrastructure during processing.
faqs.watermarkA: Standard downloads from these tools are not watermarked by JoinMyPDF.
faqs.mobileQ: Can I use this on a phone or tablet?
faqs.mobileA: Yes. The interface is responsive and works on modern mobile browsers.
faqs.variantSameQ: Is this the same {toolTitle} experience as your main page?
faqs.variantSameA: Yes—the same controls and privacy model. This page uses clearer wording for a specific situation so you know what to expect before you start.
faqs.variantAngleDefault: We keep the flow short and highlight the controls that matter for your scenario.
faqs.variantBackendQ: Does this variant use a different backend than the main tool?
faqs.variantBackendA: No. You are still running the same in-browser workflow. Guidance and on-page context are tuned to your intent.
faqs.variantMainPageA: Use the navigation link to /tools/{slug}/ for the all-purpose entry point.

Compare.metaDescription: A candid comparison: where JoinMyPDF wins on privacy and speed, and where larger suites still make sense.
Compare.title: How we compare
Compare.intro: JoinMyPDF is not trying to clone every feature of giant PDF suites. We optimize for privacy, predictable UX, and fast everyday jobs.

processing.join: Core tools run locally in the browser session.

watermarks.join: No watermark on standard outputs for supported flows.

breadth.join: Focused merge / split / compress / image conversion.

bestFor.join: Teams with strict data handling and one-off power users.

Privacy.metaDescription: How JoinMyPDF handles files: local browser processing, no watermark on standard output, and clear expectations for sensitive documents.
Privacy.title: Privacy & security
Privacy.p1: JoinMyPDF is designed around local processing: merge, split, compress, and common conversions run in your browser session. Your files are not sent to JoinMyPDF servers to execute those operations.
Privacy.p2: Downloads are generated on-device. Standard outputs from these tools are not watermarked by JoinMyPDF.
Privacy.p3: Limits still apply: browser memory, device performance, and PDF protections (passwords, restrictions) can block some tasks. Very large files may fail on low-memory devices—split first, then process in batches.
Privacy.p4: Analytics may collect usage signals (for example Google Analytics) to understand which tools help users succeed. Configure an ad/analytics blocker if your policy requires it.

PrivacyFirst.metaTitle: Privacy First — Private PDF Tools & Secure Processing
PrivacyFirst.metaDescription: JoinMyPDF runs private PDF tools in your browser. No-upload PDF editing: merge, redact, flatten, and more—files never leave your device.
PrivacyFirst.title: Privacy First
PrivacyFirst.heroStrong: No files are ever uploaded to our servers—everything stays on your device.
PrivacyFirst.heroBody: JoinMyPDF is built for private PDF tools and secure processing without the upload step every other “online PDF” site requires.
PrivacyFirst.trustLocalBody: Core tools use client-side processing: pdf.js reads your file in the tab, and pdf-lib writes the output locally. We do not need your document on a server to merge, redact, or resize it.
PrivacyFirst.trustMarketingBody: Privacy here is a technical fact, not a slogan. When processing runs in the browser, there is no server-side file path to breach for that job.
PrivacyFirst.trustVerifyBody: Open DevTools → Network, run any tool, and watch for yourself: your PDF should not upload to JoinMyPDF. That is the same check security reviewers use for local-first apps.
PrivacyFirst.howP1: Traditional “PDF online tools” ask you to upload a file. Their servers convert it, store it temporarily, and send back a download link. Your data crosses the internet twice and sits on hardware you do not control.
PrivacyFirst.howP2: JoinMyPDF flips the model: the website delivers JavaScript; your browser does the work. You pick a file from disk, the tab processes it, and you save the result—like desktop software, without an install.
PrivacyFirst.howStep1: You select a PDF (it stays in browser memory on your device).
PrivacyFirst.howStep2: Tools load pdf.js / pdf-lib and run the operation locally.
PrivacyFirst.howStep3: You download the new PDF from a blob URL—no server copy of your document.
PrivacyFirst.comparisonTitle: JoinMyPDF vs typical online PDF tools

processing.typical: Server-side — file upload to remote infrastructure
processing.join: Client-side — processing in your browser only

upload.join: No upload — bytes stay on your device

storage.typical: Often retained on servers (hours to days)
storage.join: No PDF storage on JoinMyPDF servers

sensitive.join: Financial, medical, legal, and personal files

financial.title: Financial
financial.body: Statements, tax packets, and payroll exports stay on the accountant’s laptop—not a stranger’s cloud queue.

medical.title: Medical
medical.body: Clinical summaries and insurance forms benefit from local redaction and metadata cleaning before fax or portal upload.

legal.title: Legal
legal.body: Contracts, exhibits, and discovery subsets can be merged, compared, and redacted without a third-party copy.

personal.title: Personal
personal.body: IDs, leases, and school records are poor candidates for random “free PDF” upload sites.

PrivacyFirst.toolsBody: These workflows show how local processing helps you ship documents safely:

upload.q: Are PDF files uploaded to JoinMyPDF?
upload.a: Standard tools process PDFs locally in your browser. No file bytes are sent to our servers to run merge, redact, compress, or similar operations.

verify.q: How can I verify that myself?
verify.a: Open DevTools → Network, run a tool, and confirm you do not see your PDF uploaded to JoinMyPDF domains. Processing should show local scripts (pdf.js, pdf-lib) running in the tab.

policy.q: Is this the same as the Privacy Policy page?
policy.a: This page explains why local processing matters. The Privacy Policy covers analytics, cookies, and legal terms.
